Despite this, Hamas continues to consolidate control in parts of the territory as Israel targets militants and terrorist infrastructure.

title: Generation Jihad | The Kharg Island Trap
description:
Kharg Island looks like the perfect target — take it, and you choke off Iran’s oil. But it’s not that simple. Bill Roggio is joined by Ryan Brobst and Cameron McMillan to discuss why seizing Kharg could hand Tehran exactly what it wants: a wider war, a vulnerable US position, and a fight on the regime’s terms.

The Islamic State’s Central Africa Province (ISCAP), known locally as the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), raided and burned down a Chinese-owned mine in eastern Congo earlier this month. The raid, which was far from the jihadist group’s normal area of operation, highlights both ISCAP’s threats to Congo’s mining sector and its resilience after nearly five years of a sustained military operation against it.

title: Hungarian FM says reports he passed information to Russia are 'foreign interference'

In one leaked phone call Szijjarto is alleged to have told Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov that he was "at his service".

title: Burundi's main city rocked by blasts after electrical fire at military store

An army spokesperson said emergency services were intervening and urged the public to remain calm after the incident sparked fears of a coup against the government.

title: US Supreme Court rules against ban on 'conversion therapy' for LGBTQ minors
description: The US Supreme Court ruled Tuesday against a Colorado state law banning "conversion therapy" for LGBTQ minors, siding with a Christian therapist who challenged it on the grounds of free speech. At issue is the constitutionality of a 2019 Colorado law that prohibits licensed practitioners from conducting "conversion therapy" on patients under 18. The Guardian Washington DC bureau chief David Smith reports.

title: Europe pushes back on US military operations as concerns over Iran war mount

title: Can Lebanon keep it together? Government faces Israeli invasion, standoff with Hezbollah

Now Israel’s ground forces are back to again take on Tehran-backed foe Hezbollah. Thus, Lebanon finds itself the second front of the month-long war launched against Iran.

title: EU shows support to Ukraine at Bucha commemoration
description: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. Russian military forces retreated from Bucha in 2022, hundreds of bodies were discovered, including women, children and elderly people, many of the corpses had been left lying in the streets. A delegation of EU foreign ministers arrived in Kyiv on March 31 to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

title: As Ukraine commemorates the Bucha massacre, Russia rejects idea of Easter truce

Our reporters get an insight into this showcase of continental style, from the ancestral weaves of traditional dress to the African creations making a splash on contemporary red carpets.

title: Easter eggs smaller and more expensive as cocoa prices remain high

title: EU ministers in Kyiv to look at justice for crimes committed in Bucha
description: EU diplomats are travelling to Ukraine to mark the fourth anniversary of the massacre in the city of Bucha. title: Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister
description: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. title: Yemen’s Houthis enter Iran war with drone and missile attacks on Israel
description:
The Houthis launched missile and drone attacks against Israel, the first direct military action since the beginning of the US and Israeli campaign against Iran that began on February 28. The Iran-backed Yemeni terrorist group threatened to continue and expand attacks until a series of conditions it issued are met.

title: Russia reportedly sending Iran drones for use against the US and Israel
description:
Reports citing Western officials suggest that Russia has started supplying Iran with complete drones after Russia was previously suspected of only providing components to improve Iranian systems. The unmanned aerial systems are likely modified, Russian-made versions of the same Shahed-136 one-way attack drones that Tehran previously supplied to Moscow.
